paper_metadata.title: What is a fair CO2 tax increase? On fair emission reductions in the transport sector
paper_metadata.empirical_scope_or_sample: Representative mail survey of Swedish adults aged 18–85; 932 observations used in estimations.
exact_tax_terms_used: CO2 tax on gasoline and diesel
paper_metadata.carbon_tax_relevance_summary: Direct survey evidence on support for increasing Sweden's CO2 tax on gasoline and diesel, explained by fairness preferences and respondent characteristics.

determinants.definition: Belief that people who need their cars least should reduce emissions more than others.
determinants.mechanism: Perceived need-based burden sharing can make the tax seem fairer and more acceptable.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Support for an increase in Sweden's CO2 tax on gasoline and diesel.
determinants.outcome_variable: Vote in favour of increased CO2 tax versus vote against.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Model 2 coefficient 0.409**; positive in full sample and frequent car users, not significant for non-frequent users.

determinants.definition: Belief that everybody should reduce emissions by the same proportion.
determinants.mechanism: Equal burden sharing is viewed as fair and increases willingness to back the tax.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Support for an increase in Sweden's CO2 tax on gasoline and diesel.
determinants.outcome_variable: Vote in favour of increased CO2 tax versus vote against.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Model 2 coefficient 0.615***; positive across all reported models.

determinants.definition: Belief that those who pollute most should reduce emissions the most.
determinants.mechanism: Consistency with polluter-pays norms can raise tax support, especially among those less exposed to car-tax costs.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Support for an increase in Sweden's CO2 tax on gasoline and diesel.
determinants.outcome_variable: Vote in favour of increased CO2 tax versus vote against.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Model 2 coefficient 0.364*; significant for non-frequent car users, not significant for frequent users.

determinants.definition: High combined score across fairness principles for emission reductions.
determinants.mechanism: Stronger fairness orientation increases support for the CO2 tax increase.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Support for an increase in Sweden's CO2 tax on gasoline and diesel.
determinants.outcome_variable: Vote in favour of increased CO2 tax versus vote against.
determinants.evidence_quote: high ‘fairness intensity’ increases the probability of supporting a CO2 tax increase
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Model 4 coefficient 0.874***; middle fairness intensity not significant.

determinants.definition: Uses a car every day or almost every day.
determinants.mechanism: Greater exposure to fuel-tax costs and transport dependence reduce support.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Support for an increase in Sweden's CO2 tax on gasoline and diesel.
determinants.outcome_variable: Vote in favour of increased CO2 tax versus vote against.
determinants.evidence_quote: individuals who use a car on a daily basis are clearly less likely to support CO2 tax increases

determinants.mechanism: Younger respondents are less likely to support the CO2 tax increase.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Support for an increase in Sweden's CO2 tax on gasoline and diesel.
determinants.outcome_variable: Vote in favour of increased CO2 tax versus vote against.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Model 2 coefficient -0.559**; also negative in model 4 (-0.591**).

determinants.mechanism: Middle-aged respondents are less likely to support the CO2 tax increase.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Support for an increase in Sweden's CO2 tax on gasoline and diesel.
determinants.outcome_variable: Vote in favour of increased CO2 tax versus vote against.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Model 2 coefficient -0.402**; also negative in model 4 (-0.412**).

determinants.definition: Has at least one semester at university.
determinants.mechanism: Higher education is associated with greater support for the CO2 tax increase.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Support for an increase in Sweden's CO2 tax on gasoline and diesel.
determinants.outcome_variable: Vote in favour of increased CO2 tax versus vote against.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Model 2 coefficient 0.485***; also positive in model 4 (0.457***).

determinants.definition: Reports sympathy with the Green Party.
determinants.mechanism: Pro-environmental political orientation strongly increases tax support.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Support for an increase in Sweden's CO2 tax on gasoline and diesel.
determinants.outcome_variable: Vote in favour of increased CO2 tax versus vote against.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Model 2 coefficient 1.290***; also positive in model 4 (1.282***).

determinants.mechanism: Rural residence likely reflects greater car dependence and higher perceived tax burden.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Support for an increase in Sweden's CO2 tax on gasoline and diesel.
determinants.outcome_variable: Vote in favour of increased CO2 tax versus vote against.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Model 2 coefficient -0.661**; also negative in model 4 (-0.666**).

exclusions_or_uncertainties.issue: More info on effects of climate change
exclusions_or_uncertainties.reason: Experimental information treatment in model 1, but not statistically significant for CO2 tax support.
